photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: white-painted brick facade with bright green storefront framing
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Small storefront appears active and maintained, with decorative paint and mural accents on the facade.
photo_observations.window_details: Ground floor is composed of multiple glazed storefront bays and doors beneath a false-front parapet; no upper windows are visible on the facade.
photo_observations.door_details: One side entry and one central glazed double-door storefront entry.
photo_observations.cornice_details: False-front parapet with simple coping and striped mini-awning.
photo_observations.chimney_details: No chimney visible on the front-facing elevation.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Painted brick patterning and hand-painted floral wall art add character to the otherwise plain false front.
photo_observations.roof_type_observed: False-front roofline with simple rear gable
photo_observations.storefront_description: Compact market storefront with multiple glazed openings and bright painted trim.
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Facade has been simplified into a one-storey-looking false front with modern glazing and paint treatment.
photo_observations.notes: This is one of the clearest full-frontal photos in the batch.

storefront_description: Compact market storefront with multiple glazed openings and bright painted trim.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 2 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its converted house-form, row typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 4, 0 window bays from lower to upper levels, using storefront, and 2 principal entrances.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: The property was purpose-built for commercial use on the ground floor, which has been modified through subsequent ground floor altera. The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Second Empire and other Victorian architectural styles.

facade_description: Compact two-storey commercial building presenting a false-front parapet that conceals a rear gable roof. The white-painted brick facade is enlivened by bright green storefront framing, hand-painted floral wall art, and a striped mini-awning. Four glazed storefront bays and two entries fill the ground floor; no upper windows are visible from the street. Currently occupied by 6x8 Market. One of the clearest full-frontal photos in the survey batch confirms the false-front reads as a single-storey commercial shopfront despite the two-storey structure behind.
heritage_summary: The property was purpose-built for commercial use on the ground floor, which has been modified through subsequent ground floor altera. The property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Second Empire and other Victorian architectural styles.
